heroDesc: Integrated machining for irregular and complex geometry
bodyTitle: Complex Part Machining with multi-datum process planning
bodyDesc: Complex parts may include freeform surfaces, angled holes, thin walls, deep cavities, local undercuts or multiple processes, so they cannot be evaluated by a single process label. Engineering review focuses on datum strategy, machining sequence, deformation risk, inspection access and finishing allowance.

panels.title: Process Route
panels.subtitle: Step-by-step Review
panels.body: Combine CNC, EDM, wire cutting, 3D printing or post-machining as needed.

panels.title: Risk Control
panels.subtitle: Deformation First
panels.body: Thin walls, long overhangs and repeated setups affect stability.

panels.title: Inspection
panels.subtitle: Datum Alignment
panels.body: Critical features need measurable datums and fixture planning.

tableRows: Locating holes, mating faces, key slots
Depends on material, fixture and part rigidity
May require stress relief or forming correction
Inspection datum should be defined on drawing

steps.title: Geometry breakdown
steps.body: Identify surfaces, undercuts, deep cavities, thin walls and key datums.

steps.title: Process route planning
steps.body: Define process, setup order, allowance and inspection points.

steps.title: Trial and verification
steps.body: Validate high-risk geometry and critical dimensions first.

steps.title: Batch replication
steps.body: Scale after fixtures, programs and inspection methods are stable.

asideCta.title: Need a Manufacturing Plan?
asideCta.body: After drawings are submitted, engineers can review geometry, material and volume to suggest a suitable process route.

related.title: Related Process Recommendations
related.desc: Depending on geometry, material and delivery stage, other processes can be combined into a complete manufacturing flow.

items.title: CNC Machining
items.body: Supports multi-axis milling, turning and datum machining.

items.title: Metal 3D Printing
items.body: Useful for internal channels difficult to machine subtractively.

faq.title: Complex Part Machining FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Complex Part Machining suitable for?
items.a: Complex Part Machining is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Complex Part Machining quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Complex Part Machining?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
